# PRODUCT_GOVERNANCE.md
# Project-layer file -- product governance configuration
# Generated by: npx aec init
# Read by: /generate-dpia-brief, /generate-security-assessment,
#           /generate-iso27001, /update-iso27001, /assess-security-compliance
#
# Fill in all sections during OT-1 before running any governance commands.
# This file is the source of truth for governance decisions that cannot
# come from the codebase. It is never overwritten by any command.
# Update it manually when decisions change and commit via PR.

---

## Product identity

```
Product name:              [e.g. Customer Care AI]
Product version:           [e.g. 1.0]
Product owner:             [name and email]
Tech Lead:                 [name and email]
Security Lead:             [name and email -- or "Contact: [team]"]
Data Protection Officer:   [name and email -- or "Contact: [team]"]
Development team:          [team name]
Repository:                [GitHub URL]
Deployment environment:    [Azure / GCP / AWS / On-premises / Hybrid]
Deployment region:         [e.g. Azure North Europe (Dublin) -- EU]
Go-live date:              [date or "Not yet live"]
```

---

## Applicable regulations

List every regulation that applies to this product.
Remove rows that do not apply. Add rows if needed.

```
GDPR:          Yes / No
  Lawful basis (Art 6):   [e.g. Legitimate interests / Contract / Consent]
  Special category data:  Yes / No
  DPO review required:    Yes / No

NIS2:          Yes / No
  Entity type:            [Essential / Important / Not applicable]
  Sector:                 [e.g. Digital infrastructure / Telecoms]

DORA:          Yes / No
  Customer sector:        [e.g. Financial services / Not applicable]

EU AI Act:     Yes / No
  Risk category:          [Minimal / Limited / High / Unacceptable]
  Transparency required:  Yes / No (users informed they interact with AI)
  Human oversight:        Yes / No

ePrivacy:      Yes / No
  Scope:                  [e.g. Communication metadata / Not applicable]
```

---

## Data retention decisions

These are business decisions that must be made by the Data Owner and DPO.
Fill in each entity from DATA_MODEL.md. Add rows as the data model grows.

| Data entity | Retention period | Retention basis | Deletion method | Approved by | Date |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [entity name] | [e.g. 90 days / 7 years / Account lifetime + 2 years] | [e.g. Legal obligation / Legitimate interest] | [e.g. Automatic purge / Manual on request] | [name] | [date] |

---

## Lawful basis per data processing activity

For each processing activity, state the GDPR Article 6 lawful basis.
This is required for the DPIA brief and ISO 27001 SoA.

| Processing activity | Data categories | Lawful basis | Documented where | Approved by |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [e.g. Customer query processing] | [e.g. Communication content, account data] | [e.g. Contract Art 6(1)(b)] | [e.g. Privacy notice URL] | [DPO name] |
| [e.g. Audit logging] | [e.g. User IDs, timestamps] | [e.g. Legitimate interests Art 6(1)(f)] | [e.g. Privacy notice URL] | [DPO name] |

---

## Service Level Agreements

```
Availability target:       [e.g. 99.9% / 99.5%]
RTO (Recovery Time):       [e.g. 4 hours]
RPO (Recovery Point):      [e.g. 1 hour]
Incident response SLA:     [e.g. P1: 15 min, P2: 1 hour, P3: 4 hours]
GDPR breach notification:  72 hours to supervisory authority (GDPR Art 33)
Customer notification SLA: [e.g. 24 hours after confirmed breach]
Support hours:             [e.g. Business hours CET / 24x7]
```

---

## Third-party processors and DPA status

Add every external system from INTEGRATION_MAP.md that processes personal data.
Update DPA status when agreements are confirmed.

| Processor | Service | Data shared | Location | DPA signed | DPA date | Sub-processors approved | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [e.g. Azure OpenAI] | LLM inference | Prompt content (may contain PII) | EU (Sweden) | Yes | [date] | Microsoft | Opt-out of training: Yes |
| [e.g. Langfuse] | Observability | Trace data (may contain PII) | [location] | [Yes/No] | [date] | [list] | |

---

## Security certifications

```
ISO 27001:
  Status:              [Certified / In progress / Planned / Not applicable]
  Certificate number:  [if certified]
  Scope:               [what is covered]
  Certification body:  [e.g. DNV / BSI / Bureau Veritas]
  Certificate expiry:  [date]
  Surveillance audit:  [next date]

SOC 2 Type II:
  Status:              [Available / In progress / Not applicable]
  Report date:         [date]
  Trust principles:    [Security / Availability / Confidentiality]

Penetration testing:
  Last test date:      [date]
  Provider:            [company name]
  Scope:               [what was tested]
  Report available:    [Yes / No / On request under NDA]
  Critical findings:   [Resolved / None / [number] open]
  Next scheduled:      [date]
```

---

## Contacts for compliance activities

```
Information Security Officer:  [name, email]
Data Protection Officer:        [name, email -- or external DPO service]
Supervisory authority:          [e.g. Datatilsynet (Norway) / IMY (Sweden)]
Supervisory authority contact:  [URL or email]
Incident response contact:      [name, email, phone]
Legal counsel:                  [name or team]
Compliance owner:               [name, email]
```

---

## Customer confidentiality options

Document what you have confirmed is available -- not aspirational.

```
EU data residency only:          [Yes / No / Optional]
Country-specific residency:      [list countries if applicable]
Dedicated tenant:                [Yes / No / On request]
Customer-managed encryption:     [Yes / No / Roadmap]
Audit log access for customer:   [Yes / No / On request]
Data deletion on contract end:   [Yes / No / SLA: X days]
On-premises deployment:          [Yes / No / Roadmap]
Private AI model deployment:     [Yes / No / Roadmap]
```

---

## AI-specific governance

```
AI transparency disclosure:
  Users informed they interact with AI:  [Yes / No]
  Disclosure method:                     [e.g. UI banner / Terms of service]
  Human escalation available:            [Yes / No]

LLM provider data handling:
  Provider:                    [e.g. Azure OpenAI / Anthropic / Google]
  Model:                       [e.g. GPT-4o / Claude 3.5]
  Prompt data retained by provider:  [Yes / No / duration]
  Used for model training:           [Yes / No / Opted out]
  Data residency confirmed:          [EU / Global / Specific region]

Human-in-the-loop gates:
  Gate C01 (spec approval):     Required for all stories in Active modules
  Gate D01 (PR merge):          Required for all merges to main
  [add other gates as relevant]
```

---

## Version and review

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| File owner | Tech Lead |
| Review cadence | Annually or when governance decisions change |
| Last reviewed | [date] |
| Next review | [date + 12 months] |
| Change process | PR to main -- DPO and Security Lead approve changes to Section 3 and 4 |
